questions.text: Newton's first law of motion is also known as:
questions.explanation: Newton's first law states that an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by external force. This is the law of inertia.
Momentum (p) = mass (m) × velocity (v). It is a vector quantity with SI unit kg·m/s.

questions.text: State Newton's second law of motion.
questions.explanation: Newton's second law states that the rate of change of momentum is proportional to the applied force. F = ma.

questions.text: For every action, there is an equal and opposite ________.
questions.explanation: Newton's third law: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. Forces always occur in pairs.
